QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_48

question.text: As per SEBI AML guidelines, clients are categorized into risk categories of:

answer.text: Low Risk, Medium Risk, and High Risk based on due diligence and KYC documents
Only two categories: Normal and High Risk
Five categories from Very Low to Very High
Risk categorization is not required under SEBI guidelines

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI's AML master circular advises members to categorize clients as Low Risk, Medium Risk, and High Risk based on due diligence/KYC documents. Risk categorization is based on: location, nature of business, volume/value of turnover, nature of transactions, manner of payments, etc. Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) and clients from high-risk countries may require even higher risk classification. **The Trap:** While three categories (Low/Medium/High) are the standard, special categories (PEPs, high-risk countries) can require enhanced classification beyond the three tiers. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV covers risk-based client categorization extensively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Risk categorization drives monitoring intensity: Low Risk = standard CDD, Medium Risk = enhanced monitoring, High Risk = enhanced CDD + senior management approval + ongoing monitoring. PEPs always = High Risk or above.
